headline_summary: Reuters (Apr 8): U.S., Iran and Israel announce a two-week ceasefire brokered by Pakistan; talks planned to pursue longer-term settlement.
Reuters (Apr 8): Iran warns Strait of Hormuz remains closed to vessels without a permit; shipowners and major liners seek clarity before resuming transits.
Reuters (Apr 7-8): Maritime risk remains elevated with ~30 incidents since Feb 28; insurers and shipping groups warn of continued targeting risk absent coordination.
Reuters (Apr 4): Drone strike sparks fire at foreign oil company storage units in Barjasiyah, west of Basra, highlighting spillover risk to Iraqi oil logistics.
Market focus: Even with ceasefire optics, permit-based passage keeps physical supply normalization slow; war-risk premia in freight/insurance and oil volatility persist.
risk_drivers: Permit-based Strait of Hormuz access (risk of selective enforcement and sudden re-tightening)
Ceasefire is time-limited and implementation-dependent; negotiation gaps can re-ignite escalation
High maritime incident count and explicit threats against unpermitted vessels sustain war-risk premia
Spillover attacks on Iraqi energy storage/logistics demonstrate regionalization risk

Energy: oil_up_risk: Permit-gated Hormuz transit keeps upside skew in crude and refined products; physical flows normalize slowly.
Freight/insurance: War-risk insurance and freight rates likely remain elevated until routine, unthreatened passage resumes.
Gas/LNG: Any continued Gulf chokepoint uncertainty supports LNG volatility via shipping/insurance constraints.
FX: em_fx_pressure: Higher/volatile energy prices can pressure oil-importer EM FX via trade balances and inflation.
risk_off_flows: Escalation headlines favor USD/CHF/JPY and reduce carry appetite.
Rates: rates_credit_spreads: Risk-off and energy-driven inflation uncertainty can widen credit spreads and lift breakevens.
Policy uncertainty: Central banks may face worse inflation-growth tradeoffs if energy shock persists.
Equities: risk_off_flows: Airlines, transport, and energy-intensive industries face margin risk; energy/defense may outperform on shock headlines.
Regional risk: Iraq/GCC equities can see volatility—benefit from higher oil but face operational/security tail-risk.

sources.title: What the US, Iran, Israel and Pakistan have said about the ceasefire
Shippers seek clarity on Hormuz passage as Iran issues fresh warnings
Firefighters stop fire at drone-hit foreign oil storage units in Basra
